QSC Core Health Analysis Report

eternia-Core24-d447

Analysis Period: June 3-4, 2026 (24 hours)

1. CORE IDENTITY

  • Core Name: eternia-Core24-d447
  • Analysis Timeframe: 2026-06-03 08:32:55 IST to 2026-06-04 08:32:55 IST (24 hours)
  • Data Sources: Prometheus (grafanacloud-prom), Loki (grafanacloud-logs)
  • Active Processes Detected: audio_engine, runtime_engine, node, engine_discover, video_engine
  • Metric Collection Quality: Excellent (0% gaps across all series)

2. SYSTEM RESOURCE SUMMARY

CPU Overview:

  • Peak CPU Utilization: 37.07% (audio_engine, user-mode)
  • Primary CPU Consumer: audio_engine (PID 14792) - 31.47% average utilization
  • Total Active Processes: 18 process instances tracked over 24h period

Memory Overview:

  • Peak Memory Usage: 756.7 MB (node process, PID 9000)
  • Primary Memory Consumer: node (PID 9000) - 717.5 MB average, 4.47% utilization ratio
  • Total Memory Footprint: ~2.5 GB aggregate across all active processes

Process Health:

  • Active Processes (current): 10 processes running post-restart
  • Restart Event: Coordinated restart detected at 2026-06-03 15:16:00 IST (20:46:00 IST)
  • Thread Count: Stable across all processes (runtime_engine: ~200 threads, audio_engine: 12-13 threads)

Key Findings:

  • audio_engine dominates CPU with sustained 30-37% utilization throughout the entire observation period
  • No wait-state CPU detected - all processes show pure user/system mode execution (no I/O blocking)
  • Consistent workload profile across audio_engine instances despite process restarts (29-32% average)
  • Low kernel overhead - system-mode CPU averaging only 1.18% for runtime_engine

4. MEMORY STATE BREAKDOWN

Key Findings:

  • node process shows highest absolute memory with significant volatility (654-757 MB range)
  • runtime_engine shows sustained upward trend - 395.7 MB → 412.3 MB over 17.28h (4.2% growth)
  • audio_engine completely stable - flat-lined at 238.5 MB with zero variance across 17.28h
  • video_engine anomaly - 44% memory drop (75.1 → 42.0 MB) suggests process restart at 15:15:00 IST

5. PROCESS-LEVEL ANALYSIS

audio_engine (Primary CPU Consumer)

  • Current PID: 14792, Uptime: 17.28h
  • CPU: 31.47% avg, 37.07% peak
  • Memory: 238.5 MB (completely stable)
  • Threads: 12-13 (stable)
  • Status: HEALTHY - Sustained high CPU for audio processing is expected; memory completely stable

runtime_engine (Memory Growth Concern)

  • Current PID: 15464, Uptime: 17.28h
  • CPU: Low (<5%)
  • Memory: 411.4 MB avg, growing trend (395.7 → 412.3 MB)
  • Threads: 197-205 (stable, high thread count)
  • Status: WARNING - Memory growth pattern (4.2% over 17h) warrants monitoring

node (High Memory, Volatile)

  • Current PID: 9000, Uptime: 6.72h (terminated at restart)
  • CPU: 6.60% avg, 10.63% peak
  • Memory: 717.5 MB avg, 756.7 MB peak (volatile 654-757 MB range)
  • Threads: 12-13
  • Status: WARNING - Highest memory consumer with significant volatility

engine_discover (Gradual Growth)

  • Current PIDs: 10506, 10596
  • CPU: Negligible (<0.01%)
  • Memory: Growing patterns (114→128.5 MB, 141.6→149.4 MB)
  • Threads: 1 (single-threaded)
  • Status: MONITOR - Gradual memory accumulation (5-12% growth)

video_engine (Low Resource)

  • Current PIDs: 9789, 9878, 9879
  • CPU: Minimal (0.02%)
  • Memory: 42-75 MB (one instance showed 44% drop)
  • Threads: 1-62 (varies by instance)
  • Status: HEALTHY - Efficient resource management

6. MEMORY LEAK DETECTION

Confirmed Growth Patterns:

1. runtime_engine (PID 15464) - MEDIUM RISK

  • Growth: 395.7 MB → 412.3 MB over 17.28h
  • Growth Rate: ~0.96 MB/hour (1.7% total increase)
  • Pattern: Consistent upward trend across all observation buckets
  • Assessment: Slow but persistent growth; monitor for continued increase

2. engine_discover (PID 10506) - LOW RISK

  • Growth: 114.0 MB → 128.5 MB over 17.28h
  • Growth Rate: ~0.84 MB/hour (12.7% total increase)
  • Pattern: Steady bucket-by-bucket increase
  • Assessment: Active memory accumulation; investigate if growth continues beyond 150 MB

3. engine_discover (PID 10596) - LOW RISK

  • Growth: 141.6 MB → 149.4 MB over 6.72h
  • Growth Rate: ~1.16 MB/hour (5.5% total increase)
  • Pattern: Gradual upward trajectory
  • Assessment: Moderate accumulation; terminated at restart event

4. node (PID 8929) - LOW RISK

  • Growth: 239.7 MB → 246.1 MB over 17.28h
  • Growth Rate: ~0.37 MB/hour (2.7% total increase)
  • Pattern: Controlled growth with temporary spike to 247.1 MB at 16:24:00 IST
  • Assessment: Minor growth with temporary pressure event

Stable Processes (No Leak):

  • audio_engine (PID 14792): Zero variance over 17.28h - completely flat at 238.5 MB
  • audio_engine (PID 6077): Zero variance over 5.67h - flat at 239.0 MB
  • video_engine instances: Minimal footprint, no growth concerns

Leak Assessment:

  • No critical memory leaks detected
  • runtime_engine shows most significant growth but rate is manageable (<1 MB/hour)
  • Multiple process restarts evident, suggesting normal operational cycling may prevent long-term accumulation

7. CRASH AND STACKTRACE ANALYSIS

Stacktrace Log Analysis:

  • Fatal Errors Detected: 0
  • Crashes in 24h Period: 0
  • Log Query Result: No data found in {service_name="stacktrace", instance="eternia-Core24-d447"}

Assessment:

✓ NO CRASHES DETECTED - The core operated without fatal errors or crashes during the 24-hour observation period. The absence of stacktrace logs indicates stable process execution.

8. METRICS AND LOG CORRELATION

Restart Event Correlation (2026-06-03 15:16:00 IST / 20:46:00 IST):

Processes Restarted (Uptime Reset):

  1. audio_engine (PID 14792) - Uptime reset from ~430k → ~100 seconds
  2. engine_discover (PID 10506) - Coordinated restart
  3. node (PID 8929) - Coordinated restart
  4. runtime_engine (PID 15464) - Coordinated restart
  5. video_engine (PID 9788, 9789) - Coordinated restart

Processes Unaffected:

Four processes (audio_engine/6077, engine_discover/10596, node/9000, runtime_engine/6688, video_engine/9878, 9879) showed continuous uptime from 518k-440k seconds with no resets

Analysis:

  • Coordinated restart pattern suggests planned maintenance or configuration update, not crash-induced restart
  • Selective restart - only newer process instances affected, older instances terminated gracefully
  • No stacktrace logs during restart window confirms this was not a crash event
  • Post-restart stability - All restarted processes maintained linear uptime growth to 62k+ seconds with zero gaps
  • Memory drop in video_engine (PID 9879) at 15:15:00 IST (1 minute before restart) aligns with pre-restart shutdown sequence

Conclusion: The restart event was planned and graceful, not failure-induced.

9. UPTIME AND RESTART ANALYSIS

Restart Timeline:

Pre-Restart (Before 15:16:00 IST):

  • Older process instances running with uptimes of 440k-518k seconds (~5-6 days)
  • Graceful termination timestamps: 14:39:00 - 15:14:00 IST
  • No uptime resets within individual process lifespans (clean lifecycle)

Restart Event (15:16:00 IST):

  • Six processes experienced simultaneous uptime reset
  • Uptime dropped from ~430k seconds to ~100-250 seconds
  • Restart completed within narrow time window (<1 minute)

Post-Restart (15:16:00 IST to 08:32:55 IST):

  • New process instances achieved 62k+ seconds uptime (17.28 hours)
  • Zero secondary restarts or resets
  • Monotonic uptime increase confirms stable operation
  • Complete data coverage (0% gaps) indicates healthy metric collection

Process Lifecycle Pattern:

  • Multiple audio_engine instances (PIDs 2066, 25355, 4652, 6077, 14792) with sequential lifespans
  • Multiple runtime_engine instances (PIDs 25947, 2687, 5270, 6688, 15464) with staggered lifespans
  • Pattern indicates: Normal operational cycling with graceful handoffs between process generations

Uptime Health Score: HEALTHY

  • No crash-induced restarts detected
  • Clean process lifecycle management
  • Stable post-restart operation
  • Uptime metric integrity confirmed

10. ANOMALIES

Detected Anomalies:

1. video_engine Memory Drop (15:15:00 IST) - MINOR

  • PID 9879 experienced 44% memory reduction (75.1 MB → 42.0 MB)
  • Timing: 1 minute before coordinated restart event
  • Cause: Likely pre-restart shutdown/cleanup sequence
  • Impact: None - part of normal restart procedure

2. node Process Memory Volatility - MODERATE

  • PID 9000 showed significant memory swings (654-757 MB range, 103 MB delta)
  • Peak spike: 756.7 MB at 15:05:00 IST (11 minutes before restart)
  • Cause: Unknown - may be workload-related or pre-restart activity
  • Impact: Capacity risk if pattern repeats at higher baseline

3. runtime_engine Memory Growth - LOW

  • PID 15464 shows persistent upward trend (395.7 → 412.3 MB over 17h)
  • Growth rate: ~1 MB/hour
  • Cause: Potential slow leak or normal cache buildup
  • Impact: Low immediate risk; warrants continued monitoring

4. Afternoon CPU Spike in node Process - MINOR

  • PID 9000 peaked at 10.63% CPU at 14:39:00 IST
  • Contrast: Average 6.60% CPU
  • Cause: Brief workload spike during afternoon hours
  • Impact: None - returned to baseline

No Critical Anomalies Detected

All anomalies are either explainable (pre-restart behavior) or low-severity (gradual memory growth within acceptable ranges).

11. SUMMARY AND RISK ASSESSMENT

Overall Health Status: HEALTHY

Key Strengths:

  • ✓ Zero crashes or fatal errors in 24-hour period
  • ✓ No stacktrace logs detected
  • ✓ Stable thread management across all processes
  • ✓ Clean process lifecycle with graceful restarts
  • ✓ Post-restart stability maintained for 17+ hours
  • ✓ audio_engine shows perfect memory stability (238.5 MB flat)
  • ✓ Excellent metric collection quality (0% gaps)

Areas of Concern:

1. runtime_engine Memory Growth - WARNING

  • 4.2% growth over 17 hours (395.7 → 412.3 MB)
  • Growth rate: ~1 MB/hour
  • Risk Level: LOW-MEDIUM (requires monitoring)

2. node Process Memory Volatility - WARNING

  • Highest memory consumer (717.5 MB average, 756.7 MB peak)
  • 103 MB swing range (654-757 MB)
  • Risk Level: MEDIUM (capacity risk during spikes)

3. engine_discover Gradual Memory Growth - MONITOR

  • Two instances showing 5-12% growth
  • Growth rate: 0.84-1.16 MB/hour
  • Risk Level: LOW (acceptable for short-lived processes)

Risk Breakdown:

CategoryStatusRisk Level
Crash/StabilityHEALTHYNone
CPU UtilizationHEALTHYNone
Memory LeaksWARNINGLow-Medium
Process HealthHEALTHYNone
Uptime/RestartsHEALTHYNone

Overall Assessment:

eternia-Core24-d447 demonstrates strong operational health with zero crashes and stable process execution. The coordinated restart event was planned and executed cleanly. Primary concern is gradual memory growth in runtime_engine and high memory volatility in node process. Current state does not require immediate intervention but warrants continued monitoring.

12. RECOMMENDED ACTIONS

Immediate Actions (Next 24 hours):

  1. Continue Monitoring - No urgent intervention required; system is stable

Short-Term Actions (Next 7 days):

  1. Monitor runtime_engine Memory Trend
    • Set alert threshold at 500 MB for PID 15464
    • Track growth rate over next 3-5 days
    • If growth continues beyond 450 MB, investigate potential leak in runtime_engine code
    • Priority: MEDIUM
  2. Investigate node Process Memory Volatility
    • Correlate 756.7 MB spike (15:05:00 IST) with workload logs
    • Identify what triggers 103 MB memory swings
    • Consider setting memory limit/alerting at 800 MB threshold
    • Priority: MEDIUM
  3. Review engine_discover Memory Accumulation
    • Monitor if growth continues post-restart
    • If processes exceed 150 MB, investigate discovery service logic
    • Priority: LOW

Long-Term Actions (Next 30 days):

  1. Establish Memory Baseline Alerts
    • runtime_engine: Alert at 450 MB (currently 412 MB)
    • node: Alert at 800 MB (peak was 757 MB)
    • engine_discover: Alert at 150 MB (currently 125-149 MB)
  2. Memory Leak Investigation (if growth continues)
    • Enable heap profiling for runtime_engine if memory exceeds 450 MB
    • Analyze allocation patterns in engine_discover services
    • Review node process garbage collection metrics
  3. Capacity Planning
    • Current aggregate memory: ~2.5 GB across all processes
    • Plan for 3-3.5 GB headroom if growth trends continue
    • Document expected memory profile per process for baseline comparison
  4. Process Restart Strategy
    • Current pattern: Coordinated restarts every ~5-6 days (based on old process uptimes)
    • Continue this pattern to prevent long-term memory accumulation
    • Consider automating memory-triggered restarts if leaks worsen

Monitoring Checklist:

  • Daily check: runtime_engine memory (target: <450 MB)
  • Daily check: node memory peaks (target: <800 MB)
  • Weekly check: engine_discover growth rate
  • Weekly check: Stacktrace logs (should remain at 0)
  • Monthly review: Restart frequency and memory baselines
